fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Ohio CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Ohio - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity ohio, how to transfer CNA license to ohio, ohio CNA endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Ohio.

steps.title: Complete Training Program
steps.description: Complete a state-approved CNA training program (typically 75-120 hours).

steps.title: Pass the State Competency Exam
steps.description: Pass both the written and clinical skills portions of your state's nurse aide competency exam. There is no national certification exam for CNAs.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fees
steps.description: Complete the state application, undergo a background check if required, and submit the necessary fees.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: Who administers the Ohio CNA/STNA exam?
faqs.answer: The Ohio nurse aide exam is administered by D&S Headmaster. Contact them at (877) 851-2355 or visit hdmaster.com.

faqs.question: How do I transfer my CNA to Ohio?
faqs.answer: Submit Form HEA 6907 (Reciprocity Request) by mail, fax (614-564-2461), or email (NAR@odh.ohio.gov). There is no fee for Ohio CNA reciprocity.

faqs.question: Is Ohio calling it CNA or STNA?
faqs.answer: Ohio is transitioning from 'STNA' (State Tested Nursing Assistant) to 'CNA' terminology as of October 24, 2024. Some forms may still use STNA.

faqs.question: How do I verify an Ohio CNA?
faqs.answer: Use the Ohio Nurse Aide Registry Inquiry at nurseaideinquiry.odh.ohio.gov or call (800) 582-5908.

reciprocity.notes: Ohio uses Form HEA 6907 (Nurse Aide Registry Request for Reciprocity). Submit by mail to Ohio Dept of Health, Nurse Aide Registry, 246 N. High Street, Attn: NAR, Columbus, OH 43215, or fax to (614) 564-2461, or email NAR@odh.ohio.gov. No fee. Note: Ohio is transitioning from 'STNA' to 'CNA' terminology as of October 2024.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a STNA in OH.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Ohio.

hero.title: Becoming a STNA in Ohio
hero.dek: Learn ODH requirements, complete a 75-hour program, and pass the D&S Diversified STNA exam.

seo.title: How to Become a STNA in Ohio
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Ohio STNA certification.
